channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Microsoft to Settle Activision Blizzard Acquisition Lawsuit for $250 Million
item.content:encoded:
Microsoft has agreed to pay $250 million to settle the lawsuit over the Activision Blizzard acquisition in Delaware.

For those who may not be aware, the Delaware lawsuit was filed by the Swedish pension fund Sjunde AP-Fonden, or AP7. The lawsuit accuses former CEO Bobby Kotick of hastening the acquisition process to distract from the sexual misconduct scandals that were surfacing against the company in late 2021. Now, Microsoft has reached a settlement to resolve this matter.

Microsoft’s Activision Blizzard Lawsuit Settlement Costs More Than the Budget of a Lot of Games

According to a recent Reuters report (via Kotaku), Microsoft has agreed to pay $250 million to settle a lawsuit filed in Delaware by AP7. The settlement includes a statement from Microsoft, which claims that no court or investigations have found sufficient evidence to support the existence of “systemic or widespread sexual harassment at Activision Blizzard.” The company also states that there is no evidence indicating that senior executives were ignored, condoned, or asked to tolerate harassment, retaliation, or discrimination. Here’s the full statement:

“No court or any independent investigation has substantiated any allegations that: there has been systemic or widespread sexual harassment at Activision Blizzard [or] that Activision Blizzard senior executives ignored, condoned, or tolerated a culture of systemic harassment, retaliation, or discrimination.”

The former CEO of Activision Blizzard responded earlier this year to the lawsuit filed in Delaware, claiming that it was intended to help Embracer Group strengthen its presence in the California market at Activision’s expense. Bobby Kotick also argued that the lawsuit would hinder the company’s ability to attract new talent and obstruct its expansion plans through mergers and acquisitions. In response, the Embracer Group denied these allegations.

In other news, EA ‘hated’ the idea of a Family Tier of XBOX Game Pass, ultimately leading Microsoft to scrap development. Additionally, a recent clip that surfaced on social media appears to show Activision selling an anti-cheat tool in Call of Duty. What are your thoughts on Microsoft paying $250 million to settle the Activision Blizzard acquisition lawsuit? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about the Switch 2 release date for Sonic Frontiers: Definitive Edition. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Sonic Frontiers Definitive Edition Switch 2 Release Date Revealed
item.content:encoded:
A reliable source has provided the Switch 2 release date for Sonic Frontiers: Definitive Edition.

Sonic Frontiers was released in 2022 and has become the best-selling 3D Sonic game ever, with 3.5 million copies sold since launch. Now, it seems like a Definitive Edition is in the works, which could provide some significant enhancements over the original and is set to arrive on Sonic’s Birthday next month.

Sonic Frontiers Definitive Edition Releases for Switch 2 on Sonic’s Birthday in June

The latest report by the reliable billbill-kun from Dealabs has revealed the Switch 2 release date for Sonic Frontiers: Definitive Edition. It seems this new version will arrive for Nintendo’s latest console on Sonic’s birthday, June 23, 2026. It seems like the announcement could arrive during the rumored Nintendo Direct in mid-June; however, that hasn’t been confirmed yet.

The report also provides a price for the Switch 2 version of Sonic Frontiers Definitive Edition, with the game costing $49.99 in the United States. It seems the price has been slashed by $10, as the original version for the Switch costs $59.99. Billbill-kun was unable to find the official announcement date for the title’s Switch 2 release, and what content will be included in this version.

In other news, Sonic the Hedgehog 4 director Jeff Fowler confirmed filming has wrapped and teased the first look at Metal Sonic. Additionally, Shuntaro Furukawa has stated Nintendo will deliver a robust Switch 2 lineup to ease concerns surrounding higher console prices. What are your thoughts on Sonic Frontiers Definitive Edition’s Switch 2 release date? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about the Fable rating appearing on the South Korean classification website. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Fable Appears on Korean Rating Site
item.content:encoded:
A listing for Fable has appeared on the South Korean ratings portal, hinting that a release date announcement could be around the corner.

It was recently speculated that Fable was being delayed to 2027 after a new rumor surfaced online; however, the developers have since confirmed that these rumors are false. At the same time, players have been eagerly awaiting a release date announcement, and it seems it could arrive at any time now.

Fable Receives Age-Restricted Rating in South Korea

As spotted by the KoreaXBOXnews account on X (formerly Twitter), a rating for Fable has appeared on South Korea’s classification website. The title has received a rating of 19 and up, which means it is restricted for teenagers. The reasons for providing this rating are the following:

  • Language that directly expresses sexual acts.
  • Killing frequently with weapons, depiction of blood and bodily harm.
  • Players’ choices expressing direct criminal acts
  • Excessive use of vulgar and profane language

Additionally, the title description states, “The game is an immersive open-world action RPG where the player embarks on an adventure to resolve a crisis after villagers are mysteriously turned to stone by a stranger.”

Usually, when a game starts receiving ratings in particular countries, it means the developers are closing in on a release date announcement, and the upcoming XBOX Games Showcase event in June might be the perfect occasion. However, no official announcement has been made by Playground Games.

In other news, the Blizzard Cinematics team is helping with Fable’s development. Additionally, the Fable reboot coming to Nintendo Switch 2 isn’t 100% confirmed, but it is under consideration. What are your thoughts on Fable’s rating appearing on the South Korean classification website? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about the next Mario movie, possibly releasing in 2029. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Next Mario Movie Possibly Coming in 2029
item.content:encoded:
One of the characters in the Super Mario movie 3 has mentioned that the next installment should hit theatres by 2029.

The second installment to the Super Mario movie series was released last month and has already proven itself to be another hit for the franchise. Now, as players wait for the third installment in the series, it seems it could be another 3 years before it is released, according to one of the film’s voice actors.

Keegan-Michael Key Says Super Mario Movie 3 Arrives in 2029

During a new interview with ScreenRant at Super Mario Galaxy’s digital release, Keegan-Michael Key, who plays Toad, mentioned the third installment could be arriving in 2029. The Toad actor first spoke about how the team wants “to get everything right,” which includes all the details. The directors, Aaron Horvath and Michael Jelenic, “have a real passion” for this series.

Keegan-Michael Key adds that the director duo is “as passionate about these projects as the audience is”. Hence, the aim is to “get it right”. The Toad actor concluded, stating that he wants to “apologize”, but the Super Mario 3 movie will be “worth the wait” and that 2029 is “better than sometime.”

The Super Mario Galaxy movie was released on digital platforms on May 19 and will be released in 4K Ultra HD and Blu-ray on June 16, 2026. The new movie features returning cast members, including Chris Pratt, Anya Taylor-Joy, Charlie Day, Jack Black, and Keegan-Michael Key. Additionally, fans will also receive a behind-the-scenes look at the latest installment.

In other news, Shigeru Miyamoto has discussed Princess Peach’s evolution in Mario films, explaining the shift from damsel to hero. Additionally, Donald Glover has opened up about how he got the role as Yoshi in The Super Mario Galaxy Movie. What are your thoughts on the next Mario movie releasing in 2029? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Sony’s June 2026 State of Play being 90 minutes long. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.
